messages.content:
Which figure has an order 3 rotational symmetry?

right triangle
equilateral triangle
regular hexagon
right trapezoid
An equilateral triangle has an order 3 rotational symmetry.

A rhombus has 4 equal sides.
What is the order of rotational symmetry for a rhombus?

1
2
3
4
A rhombus has a rotational symmetry of order 2.

An equilateral triangle with all equal side lengths and angle measures.
How many lines of reflectional symmetry does an equilateral triangle have?

1
2
3
4
An equilateral triangle has a total of three lines of reflectional symmetry.

Which angle of rotation is an angle of rotational symmetry for all figures?

45°
90°
180°
360°
An angle of rotation of 360° is an angle of rotational symmetry for all figures.

Which figure shows a line of reflectional symmetry for the letter T?

A letter T is folded at the horizontal line.
A letter T is folded diagonally.
A letter T is folded vertically through the middle.
A letter T is folded diagonally through its center.
A letter T is folded vertically through the middle to show a line of reflectional symmetry.

Which set of side lengths represents a triangle with 3 lines of reflectional symmetry?

3, 4, 5
3, 6, 9
5, 5, 5
5, 10, 5
The set of side lengths that represents a triangle with 3 lines of reflectional symmetry is (5, 5, 5).

Which statements are true about the lines of symmetry of a regular pentagon? Select three options.

Every line connects two vertices.
Every line connects the midpoints of 2 sides.
Every line bisects a vertex angle.
Every line bisects a side.
Every line is perpendicular to a side.

The three true statements about the lines of symmetry of a regular pentagon are:

1. Every line connects two vertices.
2. Every line bisects a vertex angle.
3. Every line bisects a side.

How can a regular hexagon be folded to show that it has reflectional symmetry?

Fold the hexagon along a line from a vertex to the midpoint of an opposite side.
Fold the hexagon along a line connecting the two midpoints of adjacent sides.
Fold the hexagon along a line that bisects two vertex angles. 
Fold the hexagon along a line that creates a right angle at a vertex.
To show the reflectional symmetry of a regular hexagon, you can fold it along a line that connects the two midpoints of adjacent sides.

If the smallest angle of rotation for a regular polygon is 18°, how many sides does polygon have?

10
12
20
24
If the smallest angle of rotation for a regular polygon is 18°, it means that the polygon has 360°/18° = 20 sides. Therefore, the polygon has 20 sides.

Which type of triangle will always have at least 1-fold reflectional symmetry?

right triangle
obtuse triangle
acute triangle
isosceles triangle
An isosceles triangle will always have at least 1-fold reflectional symmetry.

Square PQRS is transformed as shown on the graph.

On a coordinate plane, 2 squares are shown. The first square has points P (negative 3, 5), Q (negative 1, 3), R (negative 3, 1), S (negative 5, 3). The second square has points P prime (negative 5, negative 3), Q prime (negative 3, negative 1), R prime (negative 1, negative 3), S prime (negative 3, negative 5).
Which rule describes the transformation?

R0, 90°
R0, 180°
R0, 270°
R0, 360°
The rule that describes the transformation is R0, 90°. It represents a rotation of 90 degrees counterclockwise around the origin (0,0).

Polygon LMNOP was transformed to create polygon L'M'N'O'P'.2 pentagons have identical side lengths and angle measures. The points are labeled with the same letters.

Which angle corresponds to AngleN?

Angle
Angle
Angle
Angle

The question seems to be incomplete or ambiguous, as there are no options or additional information provided to determine which angle corresponds to Angle N in the transformed polygon. Could you please provide more details or options to help identify Angle N?

A transformation of ΔDEF results in ΔD'E'F'.

2 triangles with identical side lengths and angle measures are shown. The second triangle is rotated to the left.
Which transformation maps the pre-image to the image?

The transformation is a dilation.
The transformation is a reflection.
The transformation is a rotation.
The transformation is a translation.
The transformation that maps the pre-image triangle ΔDEF to the image triangle ΔD'E'F' is a rotation.

Triangle EFG has vertices E(–3, 4), F(–5, –1), and G(1, 1). The triangle is translated so that the coordinates of the image are E’(–1, 0), F’(–3, –5), and G’(3, –3).

Which rule was used to translate the image?

T4, –4(x, y)
T–4, –4(x, y)
T2, –4(x, y)
T–2, –4(x, y)
The correct translation rule used to translate the image is T2, -4(x, y).

On a coordinate plane, 5 trapezoids are shown. Trapezoid L M N P is in quadrant 2 and has points (negative 2, 3), (negative 2, 6), (negative 1, 6), and (negative 1, 4). Trapezoid B is reflected in quadrant 2. Trapezoid C is reflected across the y-axis. Trapezoid D is reflected into quadrant 4. Trapezoid A is reflected across the x-axis.
Which figure represents the image of trapezoid LMNP after a reflection across the x-axis?

figure A
figure B
figure C
figure D
Figure B represents the image of trapezoid LMNP after a reflection across the x-axis.

On a coordinate plane, 3 triangles are shown. Triangle A B C has points (5, negative 5), (1, negative 2), (1, negative 5). Triangle A prime B prime C prime has points (5, 5), (1, 2), (1, 5). Triangle A double-prime B double-prime C double prime has points (negative 1, 3), (negative 5, 0), (negative 5, 3).
Which rule describes the composition of transformations that maps ΔABC to ΔA"B"C"?

Translation of negative 6 units x, negative 2 units y composition reflection across the x-axis
Reflection across the x-axis composition translation of negative 6 units x, negative 2 units y
Translation of negative 6 units x, negative 2 units y composition 90 degree rotation about point 0
90 degree rotation about point 0 translation of negative 6 units x, negative 2 units y

The composition of transformations that maps triangle ABC to triangle A"B"C" is:

Reflection across the x-axis composition translation of negative 6 units x, negative 2 units y

Rectangle A'B'C'D' is the image of rectangle ABCD after it has been translated according to the rule T–4, 3(x, y).

On a coordinate plane, a rectangles has points A prime (negative 5, 4), B prime (3, 4), C prime (3, 1), and D prime (negative 5, 1).
Which points are vertices of the pre-image, rectangle ABCD? Select four options.

(–1, –2)
(7, 1)
(–1, 7)
(–1, 1)
(7, –2)

The points that are vertices of the pre-image, rectangle ABCD, are:

(-1, 1)
(-1, 7)
(7, 1)
(7, -2)

Triangle XYZ with vertices X(0, 0), Y(0, –2), and Z(–2, –2) is rotated to create the image triangle X'(0, 0),
Y'(2, 0), and Z'(2, –2).

Which rules could describe the rotation? Select two options.

R0, 90°
R0, 180°
R0, 270°
(x, y) → (–y, x)
(x, y) → (y, –x)

The rules that could describe the rotation of triangle XYZ to create the image triangle X'Y'Z' are:

1. R0, 270° (a rotation of 270 degrees counterclockwise around the origin).
2. (x, y) → (-y, x) (a transformation that swaps the x- and y-coordinates and negates the new x-coordinate).

Triangle DEF was tranformed to create triangle D'E'F'. Complete the statement about the transformation.

2 triangles with identical side lengths and angle measures are shown. The second triangle is a reflection of the first triangle.
Point F' corresponds to

point D.
point E'.
point F.
point E.
Point F' corresponds to point F.

A point has the coordinates (m, 0) and m ≠ 0.

Which reflection of the point will produce an image located at (0, –m)?

a reflection of the point across the x-axis 
a reflection of the point across the y-axis 
a reflection of the point across the line y = x
a reflection of the point across the line y = –x
A reflection of the point across the y-axis will produce an image located at (0, -m).

A square on a coordinate plane is translated 9 units down and 1 unit to the right. Which function rule describes the translation?

T1, –9(x, y)
T–1, –9(x, y)
T–9, 1(x, y)
T–9, –1(x, y)
The function rule that describes the translation of the square 9 units down and 1 unit to the right is T1, -9(x, y).

Figure R S T U V is reflected across a point of reflection n to form R prime S prime T prime U prime V prime. Figure R prime S prime T prime U prime V prime is rotated 270 degrees about point P to form R double-prime S double-prime T double-prime U double-prime V double-prime
Which composition of transformations maps figure RSTUV to figure R"S"T"U"V"?

a reflection across line n followed by a translation down and to the right
a reflection across line n followed by a 270° rotation about point P
a translation down followed by a 270° rotation about point P
a translation down followed by a reflection across line n

The composition of transformations that maps figure RSTUV to figure R"S"T"U"V" is:

a reflection across line n followed by a 270° rotation about point P.

Cedric applied the rule  to figure WXYZ.

Figure W X Y Z is reflected across the line of reflection m to form W prime X prime Y prime Z prime. Figure W prime X prime Y prime Z prime is rotated 180 degrees about point P to form W double-prime X double-prime Y double-prime Z double-prime.
What mistakes did he make? Select two options.

He applied the reflection to the pre-image first.
He applied the rotation to the pre-image first.
He changed the size of the figure instead of just applying a rotation.
He used point P as the center of rotation.
He used an incorrect angle of rotation around point P.

Cedric made the following mistakes:

1. He applied the reflection to the pre-image first.
2. He used an incorrect angle of rotation around point P.

A composition of transformations maps ΔKLM to ΔK"L"M".

Triangle K L M is rotated 270 degrees about point P to form Triangle K prime L prime M prime. Triangle K prime L prime M prime is shifted down and to the right to form triangle K double-prime L double-prime M double-prime.



The first transformation for this composition is [________], and the second transformation is a translation down and to the right.

a 90° rotation about point L
a 270° rotation about point L
a 90° rotation about point P
a 270° rotation about point P
The first transformation for this composition is a 270° rotation about point P, and the second transformation is a translation down and to the right.

A regular decagon has 10 sides.

A decagon has 10 equal sides.

How many reflectional symmetries does a regular decagon have?

5
10
15
20
A regular decagon has 10 reflectional symmetries.
